title: Async Context, Logs, Metrics, Traces, Health, and Incident Evidence · Inference Brief: Low Cardinality Metrics
summary: The service logs secrets and raw paths, labels metrics by identifiers, loses trace context, reports healthy during saturation, and cannot connect an alert to a user outcome. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts low cardinality metrics increment in bounded telemetry and health evidence contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define request, error, duration, saturation, queue, pool, event-loop, process, and business metrics with bounded labels and histogram semantics.
Emit structured events with timestamp, severity, stable event name, bounded route, request and trace correlation, outcome, latency, and field-level redaction.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Low Cardinality Metrics · Theory Low Cardinality Metrics
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 6261 in a burst intake service near its event-loop and queue limits. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to emit bounded route telemetry and distinguish server errors from readiness capacity; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then replace an average latency claim with p95 and event-loop saturation evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define request, error, duration, saturation, queue, pool, event-loop, process, and business metrics with bounded labels and histogram semantics.
